
### integer overflow example (1)  {.smaller}

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column width=50%}

```{.c code-line-numbers="|2"}
item *load_items(int len) {
  int total_size = len * sizeof(item);
  if (total_size >= LIMIT) {
    return NULL;
  }
  item *items = malloc(total_size);
  for (int i = 0; i < len; ++i) {
    int failed = read_item(&items[i]);
    if (failed) {
      free(items);
      return NULL;
    }
  }
  return items;
}
```

::::

:::: {.column width=49%}

<table>
<tr><th scope="row">`len`</th><td>`0x4000 0001`</td></tr>
<tr><th scope="row">`sizeof(item)`</th><td>`0x10`</td></tr>
<tr><th scope="row">`total_size`</th><td><code><em>0x4</em> 0000 0000</code></td></tr>
</table>

::::

:::::

### integer overflow example (2)  {.smaller}

<!-- \lstset{
    language=C,
    morekeywords=item,
    style=smaller,
    moredelim={**[is][\btHL<2|handout:0>]{~2~}{~end~}},
} -->
 ![](/overflow-int/texfig/intOverflowEx1b.figure.svg)

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```c
/* adapted from https://project-zero.issues.chromium.org/issues/42451651 
   Windows Kernel bug! */
char *FormatNumber(char *source, short source_len) {
    unsigned short dest_size = source_len * 6;
    char *dest = malloc(dest_size);
    char *p = dest;
    for (unsigned short i = 0; < source_len; i += 1) {
        *p++ = "0123456789ABCDEF"[*source >> 4];
        *p++ = "0123456789ABCDEF"[*source & 0xF];
        *p++ = ' ';
        source++;
    }
}
```

::::

:::: {.column}

<table>
<tr><th scope="row">`source_len`</th><td>`0x2AAB`</td></tr>
<tr><th scope="row">`dest_size`</th><td><code>0x<em>1</em> 0002</code></td></tr>
</table>

::::

:::::
